title: Cookie Chain — Getting Started
content:
Cookie Chain — Getting Started

Connect to Cookie Chain in minutes with the Solana CLI, Nightly wallet, and the community RPC.

1. Point the Solana CLI at Cookie Chain:
   solana config set --url https://rpc.cookiescan.io

2. Check the connection:
   solana cluster-version

3. Create or import a wallet:
   solana-keygen new --outfile ~/.config/solana/id.json

Wallet support includes Nightly. Install from nightly.app and connect to Cookie Chain with the community RPC endpoint: https://rpc.cookiescan.io

4. Get COOK: Bridge COOK from Solana through the community multi-sig bridge at bridge.cookiescan.io

5. Explore:
   - Block explorer: cookiescan.io
   - Deployed programs: cookiescan.io/programs
   - Validator set: cookiescan.io/validators

title: Cookie Chain — Wallets
content:
Cookie Chain — Wallets

Cookie Chain supports standard Solana-compatible wallets, including browser wallets, mobile wallets, and hardware-backed flows.

Recommended wallet: Nightly — fully supported on Cookie Chain. Install from nightly.app.

Connect to Cookie Chain:
- Open your wallet network settings and choose custom RPC or custom SVM network.
- RPC: https://rpc.cookiescan.io
- WebSocket: https://wss.cookiescan.io
- Bridge: bridge.cookiescan.io

Quick checklist:
- Back up your seed phrase offline.
- Verify URLs before signing transactions.
- Use separate wallets for high-value storage and daily dApp use.

title: Cookie Chain FAQ — Cookienet vs Cookiechain
content:
Cookie Chain FAQ — Cookienet vs Cookiechain

Cookienet and Cookie Chain are synonymous — they refer to the same network.

The name 'Cookienet' is an informal community shorthand for the Cookie Chain network. Both names refer to the same SVM chain running with COOK as its native asset. Official documentation uses 'Cookie Chain' as the primary name.

title: Cookie Chain — FAQ & Governance
content:
Cookie Chain — FAQ & Governance

Cookie Chain governance is off-chain-first and community-led. Major decisions — including bridge signer rotation, validator set policy, treasury spend, and protocol-level upgrades — are discussed openly and ratified by the multi-sig and validator operator set.

Principles:
- No single point of control: bridge vault, core infrastructure, and treasury all sit behind m-of-n signer sets.
- Open participation: validator onboarding and program listings are open to qualified community members.
- Progressive decentralization: operator and signer sets expand over time.

Where decisions happen:
- Community channels (Discord and X) for proposals, discussion, and RFCs.
- Multi-sig signer ceremonies for on-chain ratification.
- Validator operator coordination for upgrade timing.

Common questions:
Q: What wallet should I use? A: Nightly wallet (nightly.app) — fully supported.
Q: What is the RPC endpoint? A: https://rpc.cookiescan.io
Q: How do I get COOK? A: Bridge from Solana via bridge.cookiescan.io or swap on ecosystem DEXs.
Q: Is Cookie Chain the same as Cookienet? A: Yes — cookienet and cookiechain are synonymous names for the same network.

title: Gorbagana Cash — For Degens
content:
Use The Chain
For degens
If you know Solana, you already understand the base experience. The apps and culture are what change.
Docs
For degens
If you know Solana, you already understand the base experience. The apps and culture are what change.
Gorbagana Cash behaves like Solana at the network level. Wallet flows, transactions, and dApp interactions should feel familiar. What changes is the ecosystem, the apps, and the community culture around the chain.
Bridge assets into Gorbagana Cash, then visit the ecosystem page to find the dApps you want to use. Swap, explore, mint, build, or just watch the cookie jar fill up.
Simple path
Bridge in. Pick a dApp. Use GCASH like a Solana-style network with a different ecosystem.

title: Cookie Chain — For Degens (Quick Start)
content:
Cookie Chain — For Degens

A fast, safer path for trading, bridging, exploring apps, and moving through the ecosystem.

Degen quick path:
1. Install Nightly wallet (nightly.app) and add the Cookie Chain RPC.
2. Bridge COOK through bridge.cookiescan.io
3. Track balances, transactions, blocks, and validators on cookiescan.io
4. Explore live programs and ecosystem dApps from cookiescan.io/programs

Before you ape:
- Verify bridge, RPC, and app URLs before signing.
- Use a hot wallet for daily dApp activity and a separate wallet for storage.
- Keep enough COOK for fees before interacting with programs.
- Check explorer activity and program pages before trusting a new app.

Fast path: Wallets → COOK → Bridge → Ecosystem Programs
